studyName: WES and RNA-Seq Analysis in Paired Sun-Exposed Epidermis and SCCIS Samples

tables.description: This sample attributes table contains sample ID, body site, analyte type, tumor status, histological type, primary or metastatic tumor, primary tumor location, and name of the centers which conducted genotyping and sequencing.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: GENOTYPING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted genotyping

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

variables.name: PRIMARY_METASTATIC_TUMOR
variables.description: Primary tumor, metastasis, or transformed cell line

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

tables.description: This subject phenotype table contains subject ID, affection status of the subject for squamous cell carcinoma in situ (SCCIS), and race.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for squamous cell carcinoma in situ (SCCIS)

tables.description: The subject consent file includes subject ID and consent information.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This data table contains a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
